title: What are the pros and cons of using Spring Data JPA's derived query methods versus @Query?

answers.junior: Derived query methods are easy to use for simple queries. @Query is better for complex or custom queries.
answers.middle: Derived methods are concise and maintainable, but limited for complex conditions. @Query offers flexibility for advanced queries, but is more verbose and error-prone.
answers.senior: Use derived methods for straightforward queries, switch to @Query for advanced requirements. Combining both is common, but avoid overusing derived methods for complex logic. Validate native queries for portability and security.
answers.tricky: Native SQL in @Query can introduce portability and security issues. Derived methods may become unreadable for complex queries. Always monitor query performance and correctness, and prefer JPQL unless native SQL is required.
